acceptance_criteria.criterion: Many goroutines Put/Get/Delete across many distinct fileIDs complete with correct final state
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: TestStripedConcurrencyStress: 2000 fileIDs, one goroutine per fileID, final-state assertion against static oracle for every fileID

acceptance_criteria.criterion: No lock contention across unrelated stripes causing incorrect results
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: 2000 fileIDs >> 256 stripes guarantees many fileIDs share each stripe (avg ~7.8/stripe); running under -race with real concurrent goroutines (not serialized) surfaces any cross-stripe data race or incorrect interleaving
New exported test function TestStripedConcurrencyStress matches this -run regex exactly

Each fileID's expected final state is derived from a fixed, deterministic per-fileID op sequence (pattern table), equivalent to a single-goroutine serial run of that sequence; asserted via reflect.DeepEqual / errors.Is(ErrNotFound) after all concurrent goroutines finish

acceptance_criteria.criterion: No regression to existing catalog tests
